title: Government Withdraws Shelter Protections Without Replacement

violations.summary: Government carries out defunding of emergency shelter programs and elimination of housing-assistance obligations without replacement, leaving a class of persons without basic shelter protection through circumstances beyond their control, in violation of Article XVIII Section 2.3's requirement to maintain measures sufficient to prevent destitution.

satisfied_duties.duty: preserve existing shelter protections against backsliding and require the government to adopt a constitutionally sufficient replacement or maintain existing protections pending adoption of an adequate alternative
satisfied_duties.outcome: preserved existing shelter protections against backsliding and required the government to adopt constitutionally sufficient replacement measures within 120 days; interim executive use of existing lawful authority ordered

ai_summary.primary_violation: Government carries out defunding of emergency shelter programs and elimination of housing-assistance obligations without replacement, leaving a class of persons without basic shelter protection through circumstances beyond their control, in violation of Article XVIII Section 2.3's requirement to maintain measures sufficient to prevent destitution.
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties
